event: Trevor Amann scored the fastest hat trick in USL Championship history (3 goals in 3:27) in a 3-1 win over Louisville City FC at F.N.B. Stadium, completing the first-ever home sweep of Louisville.
Louisville City FC conceded three second-half goals in 3:27 in a 3-1 road loss to Pittsburgh Riverhounds, their first loss in this fixture at F.N.B. Stadium.

Detroit City FC won 2-0 over Indy Eleven at Keyworth Stadium via goals from Rafael Mentzingen and Maxi Rodriguez in a five-minute second-half burst, holding third place in the Eastern Conference with 27 points.
Indy Eleven were shut out 2-0 at Keyworth Stadium by Detroit City FC, dropping from fifth to sixth in the Eastern Conference standings.
Brooklyn FC rallied from two goals down to earn a 2-2 draw away at Sporting Club Jacksonville at Hodges Stadium.
Sporting Club Jacksonville conceded a two-goal lead to draw 2-2 with Brooklyn FC at Hodges Stadium, compounding a difficult season (1-4-11 record).
Tampa Bay Rowdies drew 1-1 at Loudoun United FC at Segra Field; result drops confirmed but full scoreline details sourced from single outlet.
Loudoun United FC drew 1-1 with Tampa Bay Rowdies at Segra Field; single-outlet confirmation pending full match report.
FC Naples parted ways with head coach Matt Poland and appointed first assistant Zak Gordon as interim head coach for the remainder of the 2026 USL League One season.
Vermont Green FC defeated Ironbound SC 4-0 in the USL League Two Playoffs Round of 32 at Virtue Field, advancing to the Round of 16 against Ocean City Nor'easters.

Lionsbridge FC, who finished the regular season 11-2-1 with 34 points as Chesapeake Division champions, were eliminated from the USL League Two Playoffs by Asheville City SC on a stoppage-time extra-time goal by Tyler Trimnal.
Asheville City SC eliminated top-seeded Lionsbridge FC from the USL League Two Playoffs with a stoppage-time extra-time goal, advancing to the Round of 16.
Lionsbridge FC produced 11 professional signings this offseason, headlined by Ricky Louis going second overall in the MLS SuperDraft to FC Dallas.

Oakland Roots SC announced they will not return to the Oakland Coliseum after the 2026 season, citing venue limitations around event control, matchday flexibility, fan experience, and operating costs.
North Carolina FC suspended operations following the 2025 USL Championship season with intent to apply for USL Premier entry in 2028 contingent on a new stadium in Raleigh.
An 18-year-old center back who made 36 USL Championship appearances for Birmingham Legion FC transferred to an MLS club, continuing the club's youth development pipeline.
A two-time USL Championship MVP was announced as assistant coach of Texoma FC in USL League Two.
